architecture.summary: A tool-supported synthesis and verification pipeline for safety controllers in human-robot collaboration. The system generates candidate controllers from a design space, formally verifies them against safety specifications derived from risk analysis and regulatory requirements, and translates verified controllers into executable code for testing in a digital twin. The controller detects hazards, transitions the process to safe states, and can resume operation under specified conditions.

biblio.title: Verified synthesis of optimal safety controllers for human-robot collaboration

claims.statement: Controllers are selected from a design space of feasible controllers according to a set of optimality criteria, are formally verified against correctness criteria, and are translated into executable code and tested in a digital twin

The resulting controller can detect the occurrence of hazards, move the process into a safe state, and, under certain circumstances, return the process to an operational state from which it can resume its original task
The integrated synthesis, verification, and test approach is informed by the process, risk analysis, and relevant safety regulations for the target application
The approach assures the correctness of safety controllers under explicit (and preferably weak) assumptions
